seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building reliable data extraction pipelines with Claude's tool use enables schema-guided extraction from unstructured documents, powering applications like invoice processing and contract analysis. Guardrails are essential to prevent cascading errors from tool failures, ensure schema compliance, and validate field accuracy in production. Vex integrates seamlessly to verify Claude's tool responses, mitigating risks in agentic workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture begins with document ingestion, where unstructured text (PDFs, emails) is parsed into chunks. Claude receives a schema-defined tool (e.g., extract_invoice) with JSON parameters for fields like date, amount, vendor. Claude uses tools iteratively: first to identify relevant sections, then extract structured data via parallel or sequential calls.

Tool responses are parsed into Pydantic/Base models for validation. Failures trigger retries or human review. The final output feeds downstream systems like databases or APIs, with Vex verifying each tool_use block for schema compliance and accuracy.

This agentic loop handles complex docs requiring reasoning (e.g., 'total after tax') while maintaining traceability through tool call logs.

risks.name: Cascading Tool Failures
risks.description: Early extraction errors (wrong date format) cause downstream tools to fail when referencing prior outputs
risks.mitigation: Wrap each tool_use response with Vex.verify() using schema validators

risks.name: Parallel Tool Conflicts
risks.description: Multiple extraction tools return inconsistent values for same field (vendor name mismatch)
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-field consistency checks and majority voting on parallel results

risks.name: Schema Non-Compliance
risks.description: Claude returns malformed JSON missing required fields or wrong types
risks.mitigation: Pydantic models + Vex type consistency verification

risks.name: Incomplete Extraction
risks.description: Critical fields like total_amount omitted from multi-page documents
risks.mitigation: Vex completeness checks against schema required fields

vexIntegration.title: Retry Logic with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Automatic retry on verification failure with improved prompts
vexIntegration.code:
async def verified_extraction(client, document, max_retries=3):
    for attempt in range(max_retries):
        message = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
            tools=[extract_invoice_tool],
            messages=[{
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"Attempt {attempt+1}. Extract accurately: {document}"
            }]
        )
        
        result = message.content[0].input
        verified = verify(
            result,
            validators=[SchemaValidator(InvoiceSchema)]
        )
        
        if verified.status == 'pass':
            return verified.data
        
        print(f'Retry {attempt+1} due to: {verified.errors}')
    
    raise ValueError('Extraction failed after all retries')

# Usage
final_data = verified_extraction(client, invoice_text)
